title: Don't Look Up
logline: Two astronomers discover a comet heading toward Earth that will cause human extinction in six months, but struggle to get anyone in power or the media to take the threat seriously.
protagonist: Kate Dibiasky and Dr. Randall Mindy
antagonistOrConflict: Political and media indifference to existential crisis, corporate greed prioritizing profit over survival
tone: Satirical, increasingly desperate, darkly comedic with mounting dread

actBreaks.description: After the disastrous Oval Office meeting, the protagonists decide to go public with their discovery through the media.

validation.ruling: The restaurant meeting with journalist Adul Grelio represents the irreversible decision to go public with the comet discovery, fundamentally shifting the story from private scientific discovery to public crisis and media involvement.

actBreaks.description: The mission is aborted by corporate interests, shifting from attempted salvation to complete abandonment of Earth's survival.

validation.ruling: Randall being separated from Kate and Teddy and promoted to Chief Science Advisor represents a major character decision and plot twist that forces the protagonists into new, higher-stakes conflict as the team is fractured and co-opted by the administration.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a relentlessly escalating satire that follows classical three-act structure while subverting audience expectations about heroic outcomes. The first act establishes the cosmic threat and bureaucratic incompetence, the second act explores media circus and false hope through the mission, and the third act descends into corporate betrayal and civilizational collapse. McKay uses the linear progression to mirror how real crises unfold - from discovery to denial to failed action to inevitable consequence. The structure itself becomes part of the satire, as each act break represents another institutional failure.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script maintains breakneck pacing through rapid scene cuts and location jumps, averaging 1.2 pages per scene. The pace accelerates dramatically after scene 76 with quick montages and cross-cutting between multiple storylines. The final act slows slightly for more intimate character moments before the climactic destruction, creating emotional weight against the satirical backdrop.

incitingIncident.description: Kate prepares for observation by making tea, positioning a Carl Sagan action figure, opening the telescope roof, and rapping along to Wu-Tang Clan while the telescope repositions.

validation.ruling: Kate's discovery of the comet while working alone at the telescope perfectly shatters the ordinary world and establishes the main conflict that drives the entire story - a planet-killing comet heading toward Earth.

midpoint.description: President Orlean delivers a speech from in front of a WWII battleship, officially announcing the comet threat and the emergency response plan with Randall, Kate, and Teddy standing behind her.

validation.ruling: Position sanity: original at 59.5%. Corrected to nearest turning-point in 35-72% window.

climax.description: The comet begins entering Earth's atmosphere at incredible velocity, creating violent ripples and bright flares from atmospheric friction.

validation.ruling: The comet entering Earth's atmosphere at incredible velocity represents the ultimate confrontation and peak dramatic tension - the moment when all efforts have failed and the main story question is definitively resolved with Earth's destruction.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MAUNA KEA TELESCOPE ARRAY - HAWAII - DUSK
scenes.summary: The famous telescopes of Mauna Kea sit on a mountain in Hawaii framed by an orange sunset.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBARU TELESCOPE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Professor Randall Mindy and four grad students wind down their day at the Subaru Telescope, sharing astronomy-themed knock-knock jokes about pulsars and gravitational bursts.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBARU TELESCOPE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kate prepares for observation by making tea, positioning a Carl Sagan action figure, opening the telescope roof, and rapping along to Wu-Tang Clan while the telescope repositions.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBARU TELESCOPE - 2 IN THE MORNING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The group celebrates Kate's comet discovery with drinks and snacks as Randall examines the pictures, determining it's likely an Oort cloud comet that hasn't been this close to the sun since before human civilization.
scenes.turningPointNote: The comet discovery sets the entire story in motion

scenes.heading: INT. NASA HEADQUARTERS - HALLWAY - MINUTES LATER - MORNING
scenes.summary: Dr. Jocelyn Calder walks quickly down a NASA headquarters hallway in a sharp business suit.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA HEADQUARTERS - OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr. Calder takes a phone call from Randall, irritated about losing 8 billion in funding to Congress wanting an aircraft carrier that's also a submarine, and demands to know why this comet discovery is important enough to interrupt her meeting.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBARU TELESCOPE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Randall tells Kate he's on hold while Dr. Calder calls Dr. Oglethorpe, and Kate explains that Oglethorpe heads the Planetary Defense Coordination Office in Washington DC.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOVERNMENT OFFICE BUILDINGS, WASHINGTON DC - MORNING
scenes.summary: Dr. Clayton Oglethorpe answers his phone while walking into a government building through a light sun shower, asking Dr. Calder about the comet's size.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA HEADQUARTERS - OFFICE -FLORIDA- DAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr. Calder reads printouts and tells Oglethorpe that the comet is at least 5 kilometers wide, possibly 10.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBARU TELESCOPE - DAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: In a three-way phone conversation, Kate calculates a direct hit on Earth in 6 months and 14 days, NASA confirms the trajectory, and Randall asks if this constitutes an extinction level event.
scenes.turningPointNote: The confirmation of Earth's impending destruction escalates the stakes to maximum

scenes.heading: EXT. HICKAM AIR FORCE BASE-HONOLULU-30 MINUTES LATER-NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kate and Randall stand on an active Air Force base tarmac as a Captain directs them toward a massive C-5 Galaxy transport plane to get them to DC fast.

scenes.heading: INT. C-5 GALAXY PLANE - HOURS LATER
scenes.summary: Kate and Randall sit in the vast, empty cargo bay of the military transport plane with only a half-cut Abrams tank for company.

scenes.heading: EXT. C-5 IN THE SKY - NIGHT TO DAY
scenes.summary: The C-5 flies through the night into day.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOINT BASE ANACOSTIA BOLLING - 7 HOURS LATER - 11 AM
scenes.summary: The C-5 lands and Kate and Randall emerge from the plane's cargo bay into blinding light.

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE HOUSE - 30 MINUTES LATER - DAY
scenes.summary: Outside the White House, tourists and protestors gather, including a bearded man holding a sign about the metric system murdering his son and two women protesting that the census steals people's souls.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Randall and Kate meet Dr. Oglethorpe and Air Force General Themes in the White House, where they shake hands and sit down to wait.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE HALLWAY - A FEW HOURS LATER
scenes.summary: The President and her entourage walk right past the waiting group without acknowledging them, entering the Oval Office while advisor Jason closes the doors behind them.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The group waits for hours in the White House hallway as office workers celebrate a birthday with balloons, shadows lengthen, and they discuss celebrity breakup news while checking their phones for any coverage of their discovery.

scenes.heading: EXT. HACIENDA COURTS MOTEL - WASHINGTON, DC - LATER THAT
scenes.summary: Rain falls on the Hacienda Courts Motel in Washington DC with heavy traffic honking outside.

scenes.heading: INT. KATE’S ROOM - WASHINGTON, DC - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kate talks to her boyfriend Phillip about waiting seven hours without being called into the meeting.

scenes.heading: INT. WEBSITE OFFICES - MANHATTAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Phillip, working late at the Autopsy website offices, tells Kate to turn on the TV to see President Orlean's crisis over her Supreme Court nominee Sheriff Conlon, whose background as a nude life model has been exposed.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDALL’S ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Randall talks on the phone in his hotel room while a BASH cellular commercial plays on TV, advertising 8G service and upcoming platforms.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDDLE CLASS HOME - LANSING, MICHIGAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Randall's wife June tells him to speak up in tomorrow's meeting and not get quiet when it's time to take credit, while their sons Evan and Marshall say hello from their Michigan home.

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE HOUSE - WASHINGTON DC - THE NEXT AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: The White House exterior is shown in the afternoon.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: In the Oval Office, President Orlean checks her phone while Randall nervously explains the comet discovery, but she dismisses the threat and tells them to sit tight, refusing to take action despite the extinction-level danger.
scenes.turningPointNote: The President's dismissal of the comet threat forces the protagonists to consider going public

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randall, Kate, and Teddy walk silently down the White House hallway as Teddy repeatedly tells them to wait until they're clear before discussing what just happened.

scenes.heading: EXT. BOJO MAMBO’S SHRIMP RESTAURANT - AN HOUR LATER
scenes.summary: The exterior of Bojo Mambo's Shrimp Restaurant is shown.

scenes.heading: INT. BOJO MAMBO’S - WASHINGTON DC - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: At Bojo Mambo's restaurant, Teddy confirms they were blown off by the President and decides they must leak the story to the media, warning Randall and Kate to prepare for the public attention that will follow.
scenes.turningPointNote: The decision to leak the comet story to the media represents a major strategic shift

scenes.heading: INT. WASHINGTON UNION STATION - 40 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Kate, Randall, and Teddy rush through Washington Union Station toward their train, with Kate arranging to meet reporter Adul Grelio through her boyfriend Phillip.

scenes.heading: INT. GIANT CONVENTION CENTER - SAN FRANCISCO - DAY
scenes.summary: At a tech conference, BASH CEO Peter Isherwell presents his new platform BASH Liif to a crowd of 5,000 attendees with an elaborate presentation featuring religious imagery and promises of stress-free living.

scenes.heading: INT. BACKSTAGE - 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Backstage, Isherwell dismisses the child performers and coldly critiques the marketing effectiveness of a young girl's eyes in their promotional video.

scenes.heading: INT. BALTHAZAR RESTAURANT - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: At Balthazar Restaurant, Kate and Randall show journalist Adul Grelio photographs and data of the comet while sharing anxiety medication, as Grelio struggles to believe the reality of the extinction threat.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YORK HERALD OFFICE - 90 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: In the New York Herald conference room, Chief Editor Paula Woods and the owners meet with Randall, Kate, and Adul Grelio to verify the comet story through multiple scientific sources.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK CITY STREET - 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: On a New York street, Kate reveals to her boyfriend Phillip that a comet bigger than the asteroid that killed the dinosaurs is headed directly at Earth, stunning both Phillip and a passing stranger.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: In space, a blinking light grows larger at an astonishing rate before becoming a blinding massive flash.

scenes.heading: EXT. MIDTOWN MANHATTAN - THE NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: Kate and Randall arrive at a 30 Rock-type building and are greeted by a talent PA who honestly admits that the Daily Rip hosts don't actually know who they are.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DAILY RIP STUDIO - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: In the Daily Rip makeup room, Kate refuses the wardrobe while Randall gets groomed, and they discuss how to present the comet information on live television.

scenes.heading: INT. LANSING MICHIGAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Randall's wife June and sons wait at home in Michigan, watching TV and discussing how serious the comet threat might be.

scenes.heading: INT. STUDIO BATHROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Randall practices breathing exercises in the studio bathroom, trying to calm his nerves before the television appearance.

scenes.heading: INT. AUTOPSY OFFICES - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Phillip watches TV at the Autopsy offices, telling coworkers that his girlfriend will be on the Daily Rip with a big story.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YORK HERALD OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: At the New York Herald, Chief Editor Paula Woods learns that the White House is denying they met with Randall and Kate, claiming the scientists aren't credible.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DAILY RIP STUDIO - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Daily Rip opens with hosts Jack Bremmer and Brie Evantee discussing President Orlean's Supreme Court nominee appearing in a 1990s soft-core porn show.

scenes.heading: INT. CRAPPY APARTMENT - WASHINGTON DC - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Teddy watches the Daily Rip from his sparse apartment, telling his cat that this is the calm before the storm of all storms.

scenes.heading: INT. GREEN ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: In the green room, Randall and Kate wait with pop star Riley Bina, who is scheduled to appear before them to discuss her breakup.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DAILY RIP STUDIO - 30 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Riley Bina appears on the Daily Rip, telling her ex DJ Chello that despite his cheating, she still loves him and wants to take him back.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The Mindy family watches from Michigan as social media explodes with notifications about Riley Bina's declaration, with Marshall's phone automatically purchasing DJ Chello's music without permission.

scenes.heading: INT. TEDDY OGELTHORPE’S APARTMENT - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Teddy watches from his apartment as DJ Chello appears via video link, confessing his infidelity and declaring his love for Riley.

scenes.heading: INT. AUTOPSY OFFICES - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The Autopsy office holds an emergency staff meeting to capitalize on the celebrity reunion story as DJ Chello proposes marriage to Riley Bina on live television.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DAILY RIP STUDIO - 10 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: After the celebrity engagement, Jack and Brie introduce Randall and Kate as their final guests, with the scientists taking their seats to discuss their space discovery.
scenes.turningPointNote: The scientists finally get their moment to warn the public about the comet on national television

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The Mindy family watches from Michigan as Randall appears on television, with June's friend Keith Ollens arriving and commenting that Randall looks great.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DAILY RIP STUDIO - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kate and Randall appear on The Daily Rip morning show where Kate awkwardly explains her discovery of a comet headed directly for Earth while the hosts treat it as light entertainment.
scenes.turningPointNote: First public announcement of the comet threat, but it's trivialized by media presentation

scenes.heading: EXT. MANHATTAN BUILDING - 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Randall chases Kate down a Manhattan street as she angrily complains that their scientific warning was treated like entertainment for popular kids at a mall.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YORK HERALD OFFICE - AN HOUR LATER - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: At the New York Herald office, a media analyst shows engagement metrics revealing their comet story bombed while a celebrity segment before them spiked, getting pickup only in fringe end times sites.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The camera pans from Earth to show a twinkling comet in the distance while Kate's voice describes it as the size of downtown Denver.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YORK HERALD OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kate reveals she calculated the impact date using a diet app and shows Randall a cruel article by her ex-boyfriend titled about dating the crazy girl who said we're all going to die.

scenes.heading: EXT. PORT IN SHANGHAI - DAY - A WEEK LATER
scenes.summary: Peter Isherwell meets with Chinese telecom executives and a general at a Shanghai port, with a translator explaining his lack of eye contact stems from watching his twin drown as a teenager.

scenes.heading: INT. CVN CABLE NEWS STUDIO
scenes.summary: A news anchor reports on a scandal involving a photo that may rock the Orlean administration as midterms approach.

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE HOUSE LAWN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Reporter Dalia Hensfield reports from the White House that President Orlean sent nude photos to Supreme Court nominee Sheriff Conlon and they had sex in the Situation Room.

scenes.heading: EXT. DC HOTEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Senator Lerner addresses the press about drawing up a formal censure of the President before heading to a costume party fundraiser, revealing he's dressed as a pirate.

scenes.heading: INT. DR. MINDY HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Randall argues with people on social media about the comet while June observes he's been spending too much time online, as he boasts about his 200,000 subscribers.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY LIVING ROOM - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Randall enters his living room to find seven FBI agents inside with two black sedans flashing lights on his front lawn.

scenes.heading: EXT. MICHIGAN STATE CAMPUS - SAME DAY, SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Kate is jogging on campus when undercover FBI agents disguised as students, a blind man, and a coffee vendor suddenly tackle her and arrest her for breach of national security.

scenes.heading: EXT. “OFFICE 4 YOU” OFFICE BUILDING - D.C. - 1 HOUR LATER
scenes.summary: Teddy Oglethorpe exits an office building when three black sedans screech up and FBI agents point guns at him, prompting him to make nervous comments about skin pigmentation.

scenes.heading: INT. BLACK SEDAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Kate sits hooded in the back of an FBI sedan, angrily telling the two federal agents that unless they're taking her to the Bat Cave they can fuck off.

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE HOUSE - SIX HOURS LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The scene establishes the White House exterior at night, six hours after the arrests.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY OUTSIDE THE OVAL OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Teddy, Randall, and Kate sit in the hallway outside the Oval Office with Secret Service guards as Jason Orlean invites them inside.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - TEN MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: President Orlean admits her mistake and apologizes, with Jason confirming scientists have verified their data, while Kate bluntly states the President is only acting because the sex scandal threatens her midterm chances.
scenes.turningPointNote: Government officially acknowledges the comet threat and commits to action

scenes.heading: INT. OHIO LIVING ROOM - TWO NIGHTS LATER
scenes.summary: A family in Ohio watches TV when their Bachelor-type show is interrupted for an emergency Presidential message.

scenes.heading: INT. 24 HOUR GYM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Nine gym patrons watch the Presidential announcement on their treadmill TV screens.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - INDIA - DAY
scenes.summary: An Indian teenager watches the Presidential announcement on his phone while in bed.

scenes.heading: INT. SUDANESE LIVING ROOM - DAY. D70
scenes.summary: A Sudanese family watches the Presidential announcement on television.

scenes.heading: EXT. WW 2 BATTLESHIP - NIGHT
scenes.summary: President Orlean delivers a speech from in front of a WWII battleship, officially announcing the comet threat and the emergency response plan with Randall, Kate, and Teddy standing behind her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Official global announcement of the comet crisis and military response plan

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - FLASHBACK
scenes.summary: In flashback, Jason discusses the emergency spending package for Congress during the planning of the media rollout.

scenes.heading: EXT. BATTLESHIP - TWO NIGHTS LATER
scenes.summary: President Orlean announces the nuclear mission using decommissioned space shuttles to knock the comet off course.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - TWO NIGHTS PRIOR
scenes.summary: In flashback, President Orlean insists they need a hero pilot while Randall questions why the mission isn't done remotely and Teddy resignedly notes they always need a hero.

scenes.heading: EXT. BATTLESHIP - TWO NIGHTS LATER
scenes.summary: President Orlean announces Benedict Drask as the mission leader during her battleship speech.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - TWO NIGHTS EARLIER
scenes.summary: In flashback, Jason defends Drask's credentials while Randall questions choosing the mercenary who cursed at children, with President Orlean explaining Drask is from another generation.

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE HOUSE FRONT LAWN 3 YEARS EARLIER - DAY
scenes.summary: In flashback three years earlier, Benedict Drask leads children in jumping jacks on the White House lawn and begins to curse at them.

scenes.heading: EXT. BATTLESHIP - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Benedict Drask stands at attention in military uniform, salutes the President, and delivers a speech about earning the extra living his potential sacrifice would provide.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - TWO NIGHTS EARLIER
scenes.summary: In flashback, Jason admits he wrote Drask's line while Kate points out it's from Saving Private Ryan, which Jason denies.

scenes.heading: EXT. BATTLESHIP - NIGHT
scenes.summary: President Orlean thanks the scientists behind her, specifically naming Teddy, Kate, and the beloved Dr. Randall Mindy who will help destroy their common enemy Dibiasky.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - FLASH BACK TWO NIGHTS PRIOR
scenes.summary: In flashback, Jason explains the scientists will be the face of the response due to their credibility, with President Orlean noting Randall polls highest while Kate connects with disaffected youth and prisoners.

scenes.heading: EXT. BATTLESHIP - 2 NIGHTS LATER
scenes.summary: President Orlean concludes her battleship speech promising to address the UN and calling for global cooperation, ending with a blessing for her party and Israel.

scenes.heading: INT. CLOSE UP OF SOCIAL MEDIA FEEDS
scenes.summary: Social media feeds buzz with reactions to the comet announcement, ranging from fear to skepticism to promotional tie-ins and celebrity gossip.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - SOUTH KOREA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A South Korean 12-year-old watches and types on her phone while news anchors discuss global reactions, religious interpretations, and the popularity of Randall's haircut.

scenes.heading: INT. DISHWASHING AREA OF A RESTAURANT KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Restaurant dishwashers and waiters look at the first clear pictures of the comet on their phones.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEWS STAND AT TRAIN STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Magazine covers at a news stand show sensationalized headlines about the comet, Drask's workout, political scandals, and blaming Kate for the comet's existence.

scenes.heading: EXT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: At Kennedy Space Center, an old space shuttle and missiles are being moved onto the tarmac while crews build launch platforms.

scenes.heading: EXT. GUN STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: A long line of white men wait to buy guns at a gun store while news reports that gun stores and shovel supplies are running out.

scenes.heading: EXT. KAL-MART - DAY
scenes.summary: An older woman explains she's hoarding shovels to resell at inflated prices while shoppers wheel out carts of toilet paper, defending it as free market capitalism.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DAILY RIP STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Randall appears on The Daily Rip assuring viewers that their best and brightest are working on a sound scientific plan that will work.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Sheriff Conlon is sworn in as the newest Supreme Court Justice while President Orlean looks on and winks at him.

scenes.heading: INT. FANCY HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Randall and Brie Evantee have sex in a hotel hallway while she asks him to tell her how they're all going to die, as he receives a supportive text from his wife June.

scenes.heading: INT. PATRIOT NEWS STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Kate and Teddy are interviewed on Patriot News where Congressman Tenant calls them Marxists and questions the comet's existence, while Kate tries to explain the peer-reviewed scientific confirmation.

scenes.heading: INT. KIDS SHOW STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Randall talks to Sammy, a children's puppet, explaining he's an astronomer who studies the universe and promising that smart, brave people will keep them safe from the comet.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE HALLWAY
scenes.summary: President Orlean walks with Randall, Jason, and Kate, explaining she'll give tax cuts to the wealthy and pass a discriminatory doctor disclosure law in exchange for Congressional support of the comet spending bill.

scenes.heading: INT. UPSCALE RESTAURANT - DAY
scenes.summary: Talent agent Kathy Logolos pitches paid speaking opportunities to Randall at an upscale restaurant lunch with Brie, while Randall questions why he needs an agent.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST LAWN - TWO DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: President Orlean signs the comet response bill into law with Randall behind her, celebrating it as potentially saving mankind while also providing tax relief and religious freedom provisions.

scenes.heading: INT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER MEDICAL CENTER - DAY
scenes.summary: At Kennedy Space Center medical center, Dr. Ginnerson informs Benedict Drask that federal mandate requires him to disclose his homosexual encounters in college, shocking Drask.

scenes.heading: EXT. D.C. HOTEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Reporter Dalia Hensfield reports that President Orlean's party swept the midterms with a supermajority and record 98% approval rating, with speculation about a Nobel Peace Prize.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: A news stand guy puts out new Time Magazines featuring President Orlean smoking with the headline 'SMOKING HOT!'

scenes.heading: INT. CABLE NEWS STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Kate appears on cable news where host Harrison Telms dismisses her warnings about the comet's threat based on viewer poll results, then shifts focus to celebrity gossip.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Brie and Randall lie in bed post-coital as she reveals her wealthy background and past divorces while dismissing the need to truly know each other.

scenes.heading: EXT. DC SKYLINE
scenes.summary: Jet planes rip across the D.C. sky in formation with a deafening roar as giant letters announce 'LAUNCH DAY.'

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUNCH PAD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Benedict Drask exits a NASA van heroically in a space suit as two techs walk toward the space shuttle on the launch pad.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE SITUATION ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: President Orlean, Randall, Kate, Jason, Teddy, generals, and Brie gather in the White House situation room watching monitors as Drask radios his readiness from the launch pad.

scenes.heading: EXT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - 3 MILES AWAY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: A hundred thousand people watch from three miles away as President Orlean addresses Commander Drask over radio, with international press reporting on the mission.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOKYO CENTER CITY - SAME TIME - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A hundred thousand people in Tokyo watch the launch on giant screens.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAIRO EGYPT - SAME TIME - DAY
scenes.summary: Hundreds of thousands of people in Cairo watch the launch on giant screens.

scenes.heading: INT. MISSION CONTROL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mission control coordinates the launch as the Mission Coordinator announces all indicators are green and issues final countdown warning.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE SITUATION ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: President Orlean gives Randall the honor of commencing countdown when Peter Isherwell unexpectedly enters the situation room during the final moments.

scenes.heading: EXT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The shuttles and satellites successfully launch with bright flames as Mission Control announces successful liftoff with no satellite malfunctions.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE SITUATION ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Everyone in the situation room cheers and high-fives as mission success probability increases to 81 percent, while Peter Isherwell pulls President Orlean aside urgently.

scenes.heading: INT. SHUTTLE COCKPIT - THE UPPER ATMOSPHERE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Commander Drask rattles in the shuttle cockpit and makes inappropriate racist comments to Earth before Mission Control cuts him off for booster ejection.

scenes.heading: INT. PICK UP TRUCK - DAY
scenes.summary: A Mexican father and son watch the launch on a cell phone in their truck behind a warehouse.

scenes.heading: EXT. HALLWAY OUTSIDE THE SITUATION ROOM - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Peter Isherwell explains something to President Orlean in the hallway, cutting her off as she protests about the whole world watching, while he urges her to show her place in history.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE SITUATION ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The shuttles approach outer atmosphere as Drask radios that everything is going well, while Brie quotes French poetry and she and Randall exchange romantic words.

scenes.heading: EXT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: A massive crowd watches and cheers as news anchors celebrate the unity of mankind, but onlookers notice the lead space shuttle beginning to turn around and deploy its capsule.
scenes.turningPointNote: The mission to save Earth suddenly and inexplicably aborts, marking the failure of humanity's first attempt to stop the comet

scenes.heading: INT. MISSION CONTROL
scenes.summary: The Mission Coordinator turns to his team and asks if anyone knows what the fuck just happened.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE OFFICE - TWO HOURS LATER
scenes.summary: Two hours later, Teddy, Randall, and Kate are locked in a White House office, confused about the mission's failure, when Jason arrives to escort them somewhere.

scenes.heading: INT. CABINET ROOM - 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: President Orlean announces to a full cabinet meeting that the comet is actually an opportunity, as Peter Isherwell reveals the comet contains trillions of dollars worth of rare minerals needed for technology.
scenes.turningPointNote: The government's true motivation is revealed - corporate greed over human survival, fundamentally changing the mission from saving Earth to mining the comet

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Kate and Teddy sit half-asleep and stressed in the White House office, having eaten the snacks, when Randall returns to hurry them out.

scenes.heading: EXT. BOJO MAMBO’S SHRIMP - AN HOUR LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Scene heading only - no content provided for Bojo Mambo's Shrimp exterior.

scenes.heading: INT. BOJO MAMBO'S SHRIMP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: At Bojo Mambo's Shrimp restaurant, Teddy and Kate call the new BASH plan insane while Randall defends the impressive science and cutting-edge technology from Stanford and Princeton scientists.

scenes.heading: INT. CABINET ROOM - EARLIER
scenes.summary: In an earlier cabinet room scene, Isherwell and Nobel Prize-winning scientists present an impressive animation of BEADS drones that will scan and split the comet using nano-technology and quantum explosives.

scenes.heading: INT. BOJO MAMBO’S SHRIMP RESTAURANT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: At the restaurant, Teddy questions how drones can hit a comet moving at 116,000 mph while Kate and Teddy walk out when Randall admits the technology hasn't been peer reviewed, leading to Kate angrily confronting Randall about his compromised position.

scenes.heading: EXT. BOJO MAMBO’S SHRIMP - 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Outside the restaurant, people smash windows and fight with cops while cars burn, as Kate sits hooded in a sedan while Teddy and Randall talk to her through the window.

scenes.heading: INT. PATRIOT NEWS STUDIO - NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: President Orlean tells Dan Pawketty in a TV interview that God sent the comet as a miracle for working people, comparing it to manna from heaven while smoking a cigarette.

scenes.heading: INT. GIANT MILITARY TRANSPORT PLANE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Kate sits hooded in the back of a massive military transport plane flying to an unknown destination.

scenes.heading: INT. PATRIOT NEWS STUDIO - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dan Pawketty asks President Orlean about Kate Dibiasky, whom she dismisses as severely mentally ill with a father in rehab, while taking the high road and comparing herself to Moses.

scenes.heading: EXT. SCOTT AIRFORCE BASE - ST. CLAIR, ILLINOIS - MORNING
scenes.summary: Kate's hood is removed at Scott Air Force Base where a federal attorney reads charges against her for national security violations and inciting riots, offering to drop prosecution if she agrees to certain conditions.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDDLE CLASS KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: In a TV commercial, a worried housewife expresses concerns about the comet's safety when Randall appears as a White House Science Advisor to reassure millions of viewers about their questions and doubts.

scenes.heading: EXT. ILLINOIS HIGHWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Kate rides in the back of a cargo van without a hood along an Illinois highway.

scenes.heading: INT. UNIVERSITY HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Hundreds of students stage a sit-in at a university, chanting against BASH investments while Dr. Oglethorpe speaks through a bullhorn before being arrested by police officers.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE PRESS ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: At a White House press briefing, President Orlean dismisses a letter from five thousand international scientists as jealous whining from those who didn't make the cut for the mission.

scenes.heading: EXT. STYLISH RESTAURANT - NIGHTR
scenes.summary: Randall and Brie leave a stylish restaurant in designer clothes as paparazzi and a TMZ reporter follow them, both denying they're dating while claiming to be just friends.

scenes.heading: INT. CVN CABLE NEWS STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: On CVN cable news, Harrison Telms discusses polling numbers about the comet debate with Philip Kaj, who reports the latest Trenk poll showing 40% support.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER MIDDLE CLASS ILLINOIS HOME - DUSK
scenes.summary: Kate arrives at her parents' modest Illinois home in the snow, breaking down in tears, but her traumatized parents refuse to let her in because they support the comet jobs and don't want politics in their house.

scenes.heading: INT. THE WASHINGTON DC HYATT - A FEW NIGHTS LATER
scenes.summary: Randall and Brie return to a hotel suite in formal wear when they discover June, Randall's wife, waiting with luggage, leading to a confrontation where Brie dismisses feeling bad about the affair and June ultimately leaves.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTHERN ILLINOIS TOWN - FEW DAYS LATER.
scenes.summary: In a declining Southern Illinois town, pro-Orlean signs and bumper stickers are displayed while teens watch Drask Dark 9 videos on their phones.

scenes.heading: INT. DRINK-MO STORE - ILLINOIS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kate works as a cashier at Drink-Mo watching TV as Teddy debates Senator Lerner about BASH owning the comet and the senator's campaign contributions, while customers argue about the comet.

scenes.heading: EXT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - FLORIDA - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: At Kennedy Space Center, President Orlean and Randall arrive by helicopter to see the incomplete BEAD drones, while Randall questions why they won't disavow impact deniers and Isherwell shows them mankind's salvation drone.

scenes.heading: EXT. OLD BURGER KING PARKING LOT - THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: In an old Burger King parking lot, Kate drinks with local kids who speculate about BASH's underground bunkers and escape ships while discussing whether the comet will actually hit.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The comet's point of view shows it moving toward a small blinking blue-green planet in the distance.

scenes.heading: INT. MAKEUP ROOM - THE DAILY RIP - TWO DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: Randall gets makeup applied in The Daily Rip makeup room, looking anxious for the first time in a while as the show plays on monitors.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DAILY RIP STUDIO - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: On The Daily Rip, Randall appears tense and awkward under bright studio lights, stumbling over words when Brie asks him to clarify the comet situation, before apparently having a breakdown and telling people the truth about the approaching disaster.
scenes.turningPointNote: Randall finally breaks and tells the truth on live television, abandoning his role as government spokesperson and triggering his immediate removal

scenes.heading: EXT. STUDIO - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Randall sits hooded in an FBI sedan as Brie gets in, questioning why he broke down when she really liked him, before an FBI agent orders her out to take Randall off the grid.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY HOUSE - LANSING - THE NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: At the Mindy house, June watches news about Randall being connected to radical Never Hitters when he arrives looking terrible, but she closes the door on him without a word.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOF OF A CLOSED BURGER KING - TWO NIGHTS LATER
scenes.summary: Kate and Yule lie on a closed Burger King roof looking at stars, discussing power dynamics and the comet's reality, with Yule revealing his evangelical background and Kate admitting she doesn't care about his impact denial.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST LANSING BUSY ROAD - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Randall drives a crappy Honda with an Uber light when he suddenly stops his car, gets out, and looks up at the night sky, finally seeing the twinkling comet approaching.
scenes.turningPointNote: Randall finally sees the comet with his own eyes, a moment of stark reality that confirms the approaching doom he's been denying

scenes.heading: EXT. ABANDONED BURGER KING ROOF - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Yule expresses fear about the comet being visible while Kate takes out her phone to make a call.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUSY EAST LANSING ROAD - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Randall calls Kate from a busy road as they both confirm the comet is visible, while their viral video spreads rapidly with millions of shares telling people to look up at the sky.
scenes.turningPointNote: The comet becomes visible to the public, validating Kate and Randall's warnings and sparking viral awareness

scenes.heading: EXT. CHINESE PARK - DAY
scenes.summary: Three older Chinese men look at their phones.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A teenager looks at his phone in a basement with a fish tank and heavy metal poster behind him.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Ten people yell 'Just Look Up!' into a cell phone in a parking lot.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVIE JUNKET SET - DAY
scenes.summary: Actor Devin Peters promotes a pin that points both up and down, advocating for unity instead of taking sides in the comet debate during a movie junket interview.

scenes.heading: INT. MEOW GUY’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: The commercial actor performs his 'Meooow' routine again, receiving more likes and positive engagement.

scenes.heading: INT. CVN STUDIO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Harrison Telms reports on a massive Just Look Up concert at Wembley stadium featuring major artists including Riley Bina.

scenes.heading: INT. KIDS SHOW STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Randall tells a children's puppet show that unchecked power and extreme wealth have distorted leaders' reality and everyone will die if people don't wake up, shocking the puppeteer.

scenes.heading: INT. PATRIOT NEWS STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Randall argues fiercely on Patriot News that the comet is visible and demands transparency about the BASH mission or President Orlean's removal, while the hosts dismiss his concerns as controlling behavior.

scenes.heading: INT. RALLY IN GIANT BASKETBALL STADIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: President Orlean tells a crowd of 20,000 supporters that 'Just Look Uppers' want people to be afraid and that true Americans keep their eyes down on the path of freedom, jobs, and God.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Three guys in 'Don't Look Up' hats yell their slogan into a cell phone.

scenes.heading: EXT. STADIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Randall and Kate address 120,000 fans at a stadium concert, declaring there's no safe way to mine the comet and calling for national strikes to shut down the economy until President Orlean is impeached.
scenes.turningPointNote: Kate and Randall escalate to calling for economic shutdown and presidential impeachment, moving from scientific warnings to direct political action

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: An activist woman throws her BASH phone into a toilet while declaring 'Screw BASH! And screw Orlean!' to camera.

scenes.heading: INT. MAKEUP ROOM AT A TV STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: President Orlean watches Kate and Randall's concert performance while getting makeup applied, then calls someone to order increased attacks on the Professor and 'Marianne,' suggesting letting 'crazies' do the work instead of imprisonment.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: A YouTuber in a 'Don't Look Up' hat makes conspiracy theory videos attacking Randall and Kate with wild accusations.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Three police officers arrest a man and woman in their 20s wearing Just Look Up shirts and regalia.

scenes.heading: INT. KATE’S DIET APP
scenes.summary: Kate's diet app shows a countdown of 5 days, 3 hours, 48 minutes and 11 seconds until her diet ends.

scenes.heading: INT. UPSCALE RESTAURANT - DAY
scenes.summary: Senator Lerner meets with Randall and Kate, expressing disgust at the President's anti-comet theories but then proposing inadequate legislation instead of the immediate impeachment and global nuclear response they demand.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: People run back and forth as sirens blare in front of graffitied Just Look Up posters on a city street.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Kate calls for shutting down the economy and corrupt governments, urging people to throw away BASH phones and delete accounts, followed by massive worldwide protests in major cities with police using hoses and tear gas.

scenes.heading: INT. JUST LOOK UP HEADQUARTERS - 3 DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: Dr. Oglethorpe informs Kate and Randall that Russia, India, and China's independent deflection mission failed catastrophically with an explosion at the Russian launch complex, leading Randall to declare 'that's it.'
scenes.turningPointNote: The failure of the independent deflection mission eliminates the last hope for stopping the comet outside of the BASH plan

scenes.heading: EXT. DON’T LOOK UP RALLY - GREAT LAWN, D.C. - THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Congressman Tenant, Dan Pawketty, Jason Orlean, and a Kid Rock look-alike address a giant Don't Look Up rally, attacking Just Look Uppers as wanting illegal immigration, no borders, and thinking science is more powerful than God.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - TWO DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: Randall, Kate, and Yule drive on a completely empty highway toward the Midwest, with Randall explaining they're going grocery shopping while everyone else watches the BASH launch.

scenes.heading: INT. BASH LAUNCH CENTER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: President Orlean addresses the world from BASH launch center as the BEAD launch begins, blessing everyone except France, the UK, and India.

scenes.heading: INT. SUPERMARKET - MICHIGAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Randall, Kate, and Yule shop for groceries including real salmon and sweet potatoes while Yule steals a steak.

scenes.heading: INT. LOWER MIDDLE CLASS HOME - GEORGIA - DUSK
scenes.summary: A family of five watches the BEAD launch with grim seriousness as the comet looms huge and visible in the daytime sky.

scenes.heading: INT. HOME IN NAIROBI - DAY
scenes.summary: A family in Nairobi watches the launch grimly on their laptop.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MINDY HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Randall approaches his family home with Kate and Yule carrying groceries and knocks on the door.

scenes.heading: INT. BASH LAUNCH CENTER - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The BEAD drones launch but BEAD number 12 explodes and BEAD number 22 fails to launch, while Peter Isherwell insists they anticipated a margin of error.

scenes.heading: INT. CVN STUDIO - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Harrison Telms reports that several BEADS exploded on launch but the mission continues, with BASH referring inquiries to customer service statements about survival of mankind.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASH LAUNCH CENTER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The remaining drone satellites tear toward the heavens.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: June opens the door to find Randall with groceries for a family dinner, confesses she cheated on him in college, but ultimately lets him and his companions inside for a long embrace.

scenes.heading: EXT. DEEP SPACE - TWO HOURS LATER
scenes.summary: The BEAD drones approach the comet in space but two more fail to attach and explode into each other due to lateral control malfunction.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The family prepares dinner with turkey, salmon, and vegetables while Teddy arrives with wine and joins the gathering.

scenes.heading: INT. BASH LAUNCH CENTER - AN HOUR LATER
scenes.summary: At BASH mission control, President Orlean asks if the mission can still work with six drones down, while Isherwell confirms 24 drones are enough but won't answer how many more they can lose.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Dinner cooks at the Mindy home while Randall's sons and Teddy watch news coverage in the living room.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: The remaining BEAD drones attach to the comet and explode in a giant flash during the simultaneous explosive separation stage.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN
scenes.summary: The Mindy family, Kate, Yule, and Teddy sit down to a beautiful dinner with turkey and salmon as June welcomes Randall home and they kiss.

scenes.heading: INT. BASH LAUNCH CENTER - MISSION CONTROL - AN HOUR LATER
scenes.summary: Mission control awaits status reports while President Orlean privately tells Isherwell the Chinese President confirmed the comet is still whole, then both Isherwell and Orlean leave for the restroom.
scenes.turningPointNote: The BASH mission fails as the comet remains intact, and the leaders abandon their posts

scenes.heading: INT. MINDY DINING ROOM - MICHIGAN - 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Kate observes the comet is still visible in the sky, but Randall insists they stop talking about it and enjoy their meal together, with June suggesting they share what they're thankful for.

scenes.heading: INT. CVN STUDIO - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Harrison Telms reports the comet is still whole with only pieces broken off, admits he failed to do his job for six months, and walks off leaving the news set empty.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASH LAUNCH CENTER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Two helicopters leave the BASH space center as news reports confirm the BEAD drones failed to break apart the comet and the Presidential helicopter has departed.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEIGHBORHOOD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frightened neighbors video the comet in the night sky with their phones.

scenes.heading: INT. MINDY DINING ROOM - MICHIGAN - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The dinner group joins hands to give thanks, with Randall expressing gratitude for being with his family, Oglethorpe thankful to know them, Kate grateful they at least tried, and Yule leading a beautiful prayer.

scenes.heading: INT. BASH LAUNCH CENTER - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The Flight Director asks Jason where the President is for mission status while a Command Tech declares he's leaving to see his husband and others start running out too.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Teddy talks on the phone in the kitchen, telling someone he's never stopped loving them and that hearing they feel the same means everything to him.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK CITY - OVERHEAD - 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: An overhead view shows New York City in complete chaos with traffic, fires, violence, and sex.

scenes.heading: INT. PRESIDENTIAL HELICOPTER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: President Orlean talks on her satellite phone inside the presidential helicopter.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY DINING ROOM - MICHIGAN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. BASH LAUNCH CENTER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jason sits alone in the nearly empty Bash launch control center, repeatedly saying 'She's coming back' as the Flight Director abandons his post to see his daughter.

scenes.heading: INT. MANHATTAN BAR - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Jack and Brie drink tequila in a destroyed Manhattan bar and gossip about network hires instead of facing the apocalypse.

scenes.heading: INT. AUTOPSY OFFICES- SAME TIME - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Phillip celebrates his viral apocalypse article on social media alone in empty autopsy offices, calling out to no one.

scenes.heading: EXT. PLANET EARTH - 30 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: The comet begins entering Earth's atmosphere at incredible velocity, creating violent ripples and bright flares from atmospheric friction.
scenes.turningPointNote: The comet impact begins - the existential threat becomes immediate reality

scenes.heading: INT. MINDY DINING ROOM - MICHIGAN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Mindy family and guests continue eating dessert and debating store-bought versus homemade pie as their house shudders violently.

scenes.heading: EXT. OPEN SKY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The comet rips through the sky like tearing fabric.

scenes.heading: EXT. - INDIA - DAY
scenes.summary: People in India run and flee as the fiery comet appears as a behemoth in the sky.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT LAWN OF A HOME - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Drask fires a machine gun and handgun at the sky while screaming until Dr. Ginnerson comes out and asks him to come inside to be together.

scenes.heading: INT. UPSCALE LIVING ROOM - MANHATTAN
scenes.summary: Riley and DJ Chello profess love while facing the end, but immediately fight over his phone passcode and privacy.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY DINING ROOM - MICHIGAN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Mindy family continues discussing coffee preferences as their house shakes violently, then the room collapses and disintegrates in slow motion.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE
scenes.summary: The comet impacts Earth creating massive explosions, magma plumes, and shockwaves that split off chunks of the planet while one spaceship navigates through the debris and escapes.
scenes.turningPointNote: Earth is destroyed by comet impact - the ultimate consequence of humanity's failure

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - 180 YEARS LATER
scenes.summary: The spaceship travels through space past an exploding galaxy toward stars 180 years after Earth's destruction.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - 3543 YEARS LATER
scenes.summary: The spaceship cruises past a black hole devouring an asteroid field 3,543 years later.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - 6788 YEARS LATER
scenes.summary: The now dented and dirty ship orbits a blue-green planet 6,788 years later.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW PLANET - 22,740 YEARS LATER - DAY
scenes.summary: The ship lands on a new planet with blue grass and strange trees as deer-bird creatures leap past, and the ship's door opens.

scenes.heading: INT. SPACESHIP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The camera pans past cryo-chambers containing CEOs, politicians, and elites before the chambers calculate and open.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW PLANET - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Naked elderly CEOs and officials emerge from the ship as Isherwell reports 58% survival rate and President Orlean notes 54 deaths including the entire Goldman Sachs pod.
scenes.turningPointNote: The elite survivors reach their new planet - revealing who was saved while humanity perished

scenes.heading: EXT. EARTH - DAY
scenes.summary: Jason Orlean emerges from rubble on devastated Earth, possibly the last living human, and calls out for his mother.
scenes.turningPointNote: Final revelation that someone survived on Earth - the ultimate irony of who was left behind
